Question:
What is the maximum carbonate weathering flux (PJ) for methanotrophic communities dominated by Type II low-activity alphaproteobacteria in areas with microfragment hotspots concentrated near agricultural run-off zones?

Restrict the calculation to:

1) progressive paludification with sphagnum dominance and fifth-order regulated low-variability mainstem, with soil aggregates low stability prone to slaking under saturation  
2) progressive paludification with sphagnum dominance and fourth-order braided perennial corridor, with very high aggregate stability after wet-sieving (aggregate resistant to slaking)  
3) mature paludified blanket with persistent surface saturation and fourth-order braided perennial corridor, with soil aggregates low stability prone to slaking under saturation
